title: Cannot Run Command From Dockerfile But Can Using `buildah run`
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.
-->
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Please note that I'm running this from Ubuntu 20.04 running on WSL2 on Windows 10.

I am not able to run a particular command from a Dockerfile from buildah. However, if I create a image using a shell script instead of a Dockerfile I am able to run the command. 

Here's the Dockerfile:

```
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV CONDA_VERSION=py37_4.9.2
ENV CONDA_MD5=3143b1116f2d466d9325c206b7de88f7

RUN apt-get -y update && \
    apt-get install -y curl

RUN curl https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-${CONDA_VERSION}-Linux-x86_64.sh -o miniconda.sh

RUN echo "${CONDA_MD5}  miniconda.sh" > miniconda.md5

RUN if ! md5sum --status -c miniconda.md5; then exit 1; fi

RUN mkdir -vp /opt

RUN bash /miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda
```

The **last line will fail**.

The **really funny** part about this to me is that this actually works:

``` bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -oex pipefail

CONDA_VERSION=py37_4.9.2
CONDA_MD5=3143b1116f2d466d9325c206b7de88f7
CONDA_URL=https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-${CONDA_VERSION}-Linux-x86_64.sh

container1=$(buildah from "ubuntu:22.04")

buildah run "$container1" apt-get -y update
buildah run "$container1" apt-get -y install curl

buildah run "$container1" curl "$CONDA_URL" -o miniconda.sh

if ! buildah run "$container1" md5sum /miniconda.sh | grep "$CONDA_MD5"; then
    >&2 echo "ERROR: miniconda.sh seems to be corrupted."
    exit 1
fi

buildah run "$container1" mkdir -vp /opt

# vvv THIS WORKS!
buildah run "$container1" bash /miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda
# ^^^ ???

buildah commit --squash "$container1" "script-test:$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
buildah rm "$container1"
```

So at least I have a workaround.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run this command `buildah bud --format=docker --layers=true --isolation=chroot --device /dev/fuse:rw -f ./Dockerfile -t tomtest:latest` as a **non-root or root user**.

Please note that I also tried running this command with vfs driver instead of the overlay driver and got the same results.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
...
--> dfb22ba084a
STEP 11: RUN bash miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda
PREFIX=/opt/conda
Unpacking payload ...
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "entry_point.py", line 59, in <module>
File "concurrent/futures/process.py", line 555, in __init__
File "concurrent/futures/process.py", line 165, in __init__
File "multiprocessing/queues.py", line 42, in __init__
File "multiprocessing/context.py", line 68, in Lock
File "multiprocessing/synchronize.py", line 162, in __init__
File "multiprocessing/synchronize.py", line 57, in __init__
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory
[15536] Failed to execute script entry_point
subprocess exited with status 1
subprocess exited with status 1
error building at STEP "RUN bash miniconda.sh -b -p /opt/conda": exit status 1
ERRO[0043] exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The command to run successfully like it does when I run `buildah run`.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 amd64 [upgradable from: 100:1.21.3-1]
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 arm64
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 armhf
buildah/unknown 100:1.22.3-2 s390x
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.21.3
Go Version:      go1.15.2
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.12.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="20.04.4 LTS (Focal Fossa)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 20.04.4 LTS"
VERSION_ID="20.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=focal
UBUNTU_CODENAME=focal
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux mycomputer 5.4.72-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Wed Oct 28 23:40:43 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```

**Output of `cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

comments.body:
Hi @tompurl , Thanks for creating the issue. I am unable to reproduce this with buildah from upstream. Could you try this with newer versions of buildah ? Since bug does not persists in latest version I'm closing this issue but please comment below if something is unresolved and we can reopen this.

Ok, I'm glad to see it's not a pervasive issue. Thank you for your quick help.

I have to imagine that this is some sort of WSL issue.
